page_content:
KNES300 Biomechanics of Human Motion (4 Credits)

The study of human movement and the physical and physiological principles upon which it depends. Body mechanics, posture, motor efficiency, sports, the performance of a typical individual and the influence of growth and development upon motor performance. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI201; and minimum grade of C- in MATH113 or higher.

Recommended: KNES265 or PHYS121.

Restriction: Must be in a major within SPHL-Kinesiology department.

KNES305 Principles & Application of Exercise Rehabilitation (3 Credits) In-depth analysis of the basic principles of rehabilitation and exercise programming for common injuries encountered in a clinical setting. Basic assessment techniques, ranges of motion, muscular anatomy, rehabilitative protocols, and exercise modalities are a major focus. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI201 and BSCI202; and 1 course with a minimum grade of C- from (KNES300, KNES350, KNES360, KNES370, KNES385).

Restriction: Must be in a major within the SPHL-Kinesiology department; and must have earned a minimum of 75 credits.

Credit Only Granted for. KNES498T or KNES305.
